title: helenium acuminatum
intro: Helenium acuminatum, also known as goldeneye or narrow-leaved sneezeweed, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and reducing fever. The health benefits of Helenium acuminatum include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as colds and allergies, as well as its potential to support the immune system. Helenium acuminatum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often combined with other herbs in herbal preparations to enhance its medicinal effects.
